methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Zeromark is a seed-stage defense and security company founded by Joel Anderson that has raised $10M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 10 private and 2 public peers in the Defense & Security sector, including companies such as Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems.

The Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable approach, using the $10M total raised as a proxy base value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical for seed-to-next-stage advancement. This yields an implied valuation of $25M. The Peer-Median method was not applicable due to insufficient narrow peer coverage; while 19 same-sector companies exist in the comp universe, fewer than three met the filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparables, making sector-wide medians unreliable.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $21M to $29M, with a median point of $25M. This range reflects typical seed-stage step-up dynamics but carries material uncertainty stemming from the reliance on a single quantitative method. The narrow applicability of peer-based triangulation—a structural challenge in the frontier-defense sector—means the valuation is sensitive to assumptions about stage progression timing and the appropriateness of the 2.5x multiplier to this particular company's profile and market conditions.
